Chiffon cakes contain eggs; the cookie/cracker bakes are wheat-, dairy- and egg-free. Not a certified dedicated kitchen, and oatmeal carries a wheat cross-contact caveat unless certified GF, so a cautious celiac should confirm oat sourcing.

chef_bio.background: The shop opened in October 2024 in a renovated garage in Kyōmachi, Kanazawa, marked by a white wall and a pink sign near Johoku Hospital. According to the weekend-kanazawa.com feature, the owner turned to rice-flour and gluten-free baking after her child's allergies made ordinary wheat sweets impossible, and built a lineup of roughly ten chiffon flavours plus gluten-free焼き菓子. The owner name is reported by the source and could not be independently re-confirmed elsewhere.
chef_bio.philosophy: Make gentle, everyday sweets that people with wheat, dairy and egg allergies can eat safely.
chef_bio.specialty: Fluffy, moist rice-flour chiffon cakes

anecdotes.text: Recipes were developed in response to a child's allergies, driving the fully rice-flour, gluten-free approach.

chef_bio.japanese_sources_summary: 週末、金沢。 reports マイシフォン as a 米粉シフォン専門店 opened Oct 2024 in 金沢市京町, with 焼き菓子 that are 「小麦粉や乳製品、卵不使用」; owner name attribution is single-source.

gf_cross_contamination.text: 米粉シフォン専門店 — the shop works only with rice flour and gluten-free ingredients, but no dedicated/certified GF-kitchen claim was found; oatmeal used in some bakes can carry wheat cross-contact unless certified.

soy_sauce_wheat.text: Not applicable — dessert/bakery shop, no soy-sauce dishes.

vegan_cross_contact.text: Egg-containing chiffon batter and egg-free cookies are made in the same shop; ask if strictly vegan.

staff_allergy_handling.text: Owner reportedly created the recipes for a child's allergies, indicating strong allergy awareness.

positives.text: Entire lineup is rice-flour based; several bakes are 「小麦粉や乳製品、卵不使用」.

places.name: Hakusan Quiche House QUI (白山キッシュハウス QUI)
places.gf_detail: A gluten-free rice-flour quiche specialist (地元の米粉を使ったグルテンフリーキッシュの専門店) at the foot of Shishiku Kogen. The tart shell is 100% rice flour milled in Hakusan and the shop states 「当店のキッシュは全てグルテンフリーとなっております。」 (all our quiches are gluten-free) — the entire menu, quiche plus rice-flour breads/tarts/cookies, is rice-flour based with no wheat products made on-site, so it functions as a dedicated GF kitchen. Important caveat: the shop's shared-facility warning 「全ての商品が同じ場所で作られておりますので、アレルギーをお持ちの方はご注意ください」 is written for egg and dairy and makes NO explicit wheat/gluten cross-contamination statement, nor is there a certification. A sensitive celiac should phone (076-214-6939) to confirm wheat handling.
places.vegan_detail: The everyday menu is not vegan — every quiche contains egg and most contain cheese, though the custard uses egg plus soy-milk cream (卵と豆乳クリーム) rather than dairy cream, making it lighter than a classic quiche. True 動物性不使用 vegan items appear only as rotating limited specials (e.g. a vegan vegetable-and-soy-cream quiche and an avocado-chocolate soy-cream tart at a 2022 pop-up), not on the standard menu. A vegan visitor must check Instagram (@hakusan_qui) for current availability.
places.hours_raw: 11:00–16:00 (L.O.), lunch 11:00–14:00; closed Tuesdays (open when Tue is a public holiday); January weekends-only in deep winter
places.neighborhood: 八幡町 パーク獅子吼 (Hakusan, Ishikawa — Shishiku Kogen area, near 白山比咩神社)
places.notes: [GF-dedicated] A 100% rice-flour quiche house — the whole menu, from quiche to rice-flour breads and tarts, is gluten-free, and the shop advertises itself as a gluten-free specialist. Because no wheat products are made here it effectively runs as a dedicated GF kitchen, but note the shop publishes only an egg/dairy shared-facility warning and no explicit wheat cross-contamination statement or certification, so a strict celiac should confirm by phone. Quiches contain egg (custard uses soy-milk cream, not dairy cream); vegan items are only rotating specials. In January it runs weekends-only.

chef_bio.background: QUI opened on 1 June 2018 inside Park Shishiku, a renovated former Tsurugi-town products hall at the foot of Shishiku Kogen. The registered representative is Takada Shigeyuki (per the ISICO DGnet business registry). A local blog reports that after retiring from work at the nearby facility もく遊りん, the owner opened QUI to pursue thoroughly ingredient-focused cooking — 「定年退職して、素材にとことんこだわったお店をやってみようと…オープンしたんですって」 (reported speech, not officially documented).
chef_bio.philosophy: Promote local Hakusan Koshihikari rice, be allergy-friendly, and offer a 'body-friendly' wellness ethos. QUI's blog notes 「米粉にはグルテンがなく…食後は身体に負荷はありません」, and the About page frames the shop as 「忙しさで頑張りすぎた心と体を『自然と食』でニュートラルに戻す米粉キッシュのお店」 with the motto 「頑張りすぎなくていい」. No personal or family gluten-intolerance backstory is documented — the rice-flour choice is framed around local rice, allergy-friendliness and health.
chef_bio.specialty: Rice-flour (米粉) quiche with a 100% rice-flour almond-tart shell and egg + soy-milk-cream custard

anecdotes.text: Certified a 白山市地産地消推奨店 (Hakusan City local-production-consumption recommended shop) for its use of locally milled rice flour.
Ran a Kanazawa Korinbo Tokyu Square pop-up in September 2022 that included rotating vegan quiche and vegan tart specials.

chef_bio.japanese_sources_summary: The official qui-hakusan.com site establishes the gluten-free rice-flour specialty and the 100% rice-flour crust first-person; urara-hakusanbito (Hakusan tourism) corroborates the Hakusan Koshihikari rice-flour origin and the 小麦アレルギーの方でも安心 framing; the ISICO DGnet registry gives the representative name; a local blog supplies the (unofficial) owner backstory.

gf_cross_contamination.text: 「当店のキッシュは全てグルテンフリーとなっております。」 — all quiches are gluten-free; crust ingredient list shows rice flour (米粉/白山市製造), corn starch, almond powder, rice oil — no wheat listed.
gf_cross_contamination.source: https://www.qui-hakusan.com/products/おすすめの米粉キッシュセット

gf_cross_contamination.text: 「全ての商品が同じ場所で作られておりますので、アレルギーをお持ちの方はご注意ください」 — a shared-facility caveat, but it is written for egg/dairy; NO explicit wheat/gluten cross-contamination statement or dedicated-kitchen guarantee is published. Confirm wheat handling by phone.
gf_cross_contamination.source: https://www.qui-hakusan.com/products/おすすめの米粉キッシュセット

gf_cross_contamination.text: 「小麦アレルギーの方でも安心して食べられるように、グルテンフリーになっています」 — tourism corroboration that the shop is gluten-free so wheat-allergic guests can eat safely.

soy_sauce_wheat.text: No soy-sauce/tamari statement is published. The shop labels allergens per item (e.g. a pizza page lists shrimp and dairy), showing allergen disclosure, but savory-seasoning (醤油) wheat content is not documented — ask if ordering savory items.

vegan_cross_contact.text: Standard menu is not vegan (all quiches contain egg, most contain cheese); vegan items appear only as rotating specials, so everyday vegan cross-contact handling is not documented.

staff_allergy_handling.text: The shop discloses per-item allergens (egg, dairy, shrimp, etc.) but publishes no documented staff gluten-handling protocol beyond the whole-menu gluten-free branding.
staff_allergy_handling.source: https://www.qui-hakusan.com/products/おすすめの米粉キッシュセット

positives.text: Entire menu is 100% rice-flour and branded gluten-free; the crust uses rice flour milled in Hakusan with no wheat, and no wheat products are made on-site.
Certified Hakusan City local-production-consumption recommended shop; discloses allergens per item.
